network.overall_strategy: This network is centered on the only detailed literature-supported prebiotic route to arginine: the cyanosulfidic homologation sequence of Patel et al. (2015), in which HCN, acetylene, NH3, cyanamide, H2S, UV light, and Cu/Zn-mediated chemistry generate acrylonitrile, beta-aminopropionitrile, guanidinium-bearing intermediates, thiolactam intermediates, and finally an arginine alpha-aminonitrile that hydrolyzes to arginine. Hydrothermal chemistry is used conservatively and only for literature-consistent provisioning of simple reduced feedstocks and ammonia-rich, Fe-S-catalyzed chemistry that plausibly supplies carbon/nitrogen inputs to surface cyanosulfidic pools; the arginine-forming chemistry itself follows the Patel route. Ten distinct pathways are represented as alternative traversals through the same connected bipartite graph, differing in how hydrothermal feedstocks are routed into the surface sequence and in whether the late-stage arginine precursor is reached through the full homologation branch or via literature-acknowledged but explicitly speculative metabolism-analog branches through ornithine/citrulline. Speculative steps are clearly labeled as such in reaction reasoning, consistent with the literature stating that no convincing non-Patel route is yet demonstrated.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal NH3 provisioning in Fe-S alkaline vent setting

conditions.other: alkaline hydrothermal vent; reducing, anoxic, NH3-rich vent fluids invoked as environmental feedstock source rather than direct stoichiometric laboratory synthesis

reactions.mechanism: Vent-associated Fe-S mineral catalysis and water-rock redox chemistry generate reducing conditions compatible with ammonia-bearing hydrothermal fluids feeding surface environments.
reactions.reasoning: Used conservatively as an environmental feedstock-provisioning step because the supplied literature emphasizes ammonia-rich hydrothermal settings rather than a specific Arg-forming vent reaction. Hydrothermal vents provide H2, NH3 and FeS/NiS catalysis for general reductive chemistry, but no direct arginine synthesis is known (Huber & Wachtershauser, 1997 as summarized in ref. [9]; Glavin et al., 2025; Pol et al., 2022). This step is therefore a network connector, not a claim of demonstrated arginine synthesis.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal H2S provisioning in serpentinizing Fe-S systems

conditions.other: subseafloor hydrothermal, anoxic, reduced sulfur-bearing fluids

reactions.mechanism: Fe-S mineral buffering in hydrothermal systems supplies reduced sulfur species to later surface photoredox chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: The deep research report identifies H2S as the key reductant in the Patel cyanosulfidic network and highlights hydrothermal and sulfur-rich settings as plausible suppliers of reduced sulfur. This is included as a feedstock-transfer step linking hydrothermal sulfur chemistry to surface UV-driven homologation, not as a specific Arg-forming experiment (Patel et al., 2015; Glavin et al., 2025).

reactions.name: Surface UV hydrolysis of HCN to formamide
reactions.mechanism: HCN in aqueous UV-exposed surface settings is converted through hydrolysis/photochemical routes to formamide, which can serve as a precursor reservoir for cyanamide-rich nitrogen chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: The report notes UV photochemistry of HCN to formamide and identifies HCN-rich surface settings as central to prebiotic nitrogen chemistry, though this step is not Arg-specific. Included to connect nitrogen feedstock transformations relevant to cyanamide emergence in cyanosulfidic settings (Miller, 1986 review in ref. [1]; Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Surface dehydration of formamide to cyanamide

conditions.other: wet-dry cycling in evaporitic pool; UV-exposed

reactions.mechanism: Dry-down and mineral-assisted dehydration convert amide-like nitrogen reservoirs into cyanamide, a guanidinylating agent needed for the Patel sequence.
reactions.reasoning: Cyanamide is explicitly identified in the literature as a key guanidinylating reagent in the Patel route. Its exact geochemical sourcing is less firmly demonstrated than its use, so this connector is moderately speculative but consistent with the report's emphasis on wet-dry and photochemical cyanamide-rich surface settings (Patel et al., 2015; Miller, 1986 review).

reactions.name: Cu-photoredox cyanosulfidic coupling of HCN and acetylene to acrylonitrile

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox with Zn2+; sulfide mineral-rich UV-irradiated aqueous setting
conditions.other: UV around 300 nm; H2S reductant; stepwise reagent addition; reported yield up to >25%

reactions.mechanism: Photoredox activation of cyanide/acetylene in sulfide-rich water forms the three-carbon nitrile acrylonitrile.
reactions.reasoning: This is the core literature-supported entry to the arginine network. Patel et al. showed that HCN and acetylene under UV with Cu photoredox chemistry and H2S reductant produce acrylonitrile in significant yield, providing the backbone precursor for arginine homologation (Patel et al., 2015, refs. [3], [4], [6], [8]).

reactions.name: Ammonia addition to acrylonitrile forming beta-aminopropionitrile

conditions.catalyst: none required in solution; mineral surfaces used for concentration
conditions.other: neutral to slightly basic aqueous conditions; ammonia addition step in Patel sequence

reactions.mechanism: Nucleophilic conjugate addition of ammonia to the activated nitrile alkene yields beta-aminopropionitrile.
reactions.reasoning: Beta-aminopropionitrile is explicitly identified as a central Patel intermediate arising from acrylonitrile plus ammonia. Mineral phases are included here for concentration, satisfying the surface-agent requirement, especially because clays can concentrate cationic/basic products and intermediates (Patel et al., 2015; Frenkel-Pinter et al., 2024/2025-like adsorption study summarized as ref. [11]).

reactions.name: UV/H2S photoreduction of beta-aminopropionitrile to beta-aminopropionaldehyde

conditions.catalyst: UV photoredox with H2S reductant; Cu/Zn-mediated cyanosulfidic conditions
conditions.other: acidic photoreduction stage; stepwise addition chemistry

reactions.mechanism: Cyanosulfidic photoreduction converts the nitrile-containing precursor into the corresponding aldehyde under H2S-driven UV chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: The report states that beta-aminopropionitrile is reduced under UV/H2S conditions to beta-aminopropionaldehyde in the Patel sequence before cyanamide capture. This is a key documented step in the only detailed prebiotic Arg route (Patel et al., 2015, refs. [3], [4], [8]).

reactions.name: Cyanamide capture to cyclic guanidinium hemiaminal

conditions.catalyst: cyanamide reagent capture on concentrated mineral surfaces
conditions.other: rapid addition under cyanosulfidic stepwise conditions

reactions.mechanism: The aldehyde rapidly condenses with cyanamide to generate a guanidinium-bearing cyclic hemiaminal, installing the crucial guanidino functionality.
reactions.reasoning: The literature explicitly highlights cyanamide as the guanidinylating reagent and identifies the cyclic guanidinium hemiaminal as a key intermediate after reduction of beta-aminopropionitrile. This step addresses the central challenge of guanidinium installation (Patel et al., 2015; Pol et al., 2022 on the rarity of convincing Arg routes).

reactions.name: Kiliani-Fischer homologation and sulfur incorporation to gamma-butyrothiolactam

conditions.other: stepwise HCN homologation; alpha-hydroxythioamide intermediate collapses to thiolactam

reactions.mechanism: HCN homologation followed by photoreductive sulfur chemistry generates an alpha-hydroxythioamide that cyclizes to gamma-butyrothiolactam.
reactions.reasoning: This directly follows the Patel scheme, where the guanidinium hemiaminal undergoes HCN addition and photoreduction to alpha-hydroxythioamide and then cyclizes into gamma-butyrothiolactam. It is one of the defining middle-stage transformations in the proposed Arg synthesis (Patel et al., 2015, refs. [3], [4], [8]).

reactions.name: Final photoreductive homologation to arginine alpha-aminonitrile

conditions.catalyst: UV/H2S photoredox under Cu/Zn cyanosulfidic conditions
conditions.other: further reduction and HCN addition; stepwise reagent pulses; reported later homologations described as good to excellent

reactions.mechanism: The thiolactam intermediate is further reduced and homologated with HCN to furnish the alpha-aminonitrile precursor to arginine (compound 46).
reactions.reasoning: The research report states that further reduction and HCN addition ultimately produce compound 46, the alpha-aminonitrile precursor to arginine. This is the late, literature-supported endpoint of the Patel route before hydrolysis (Patel et al., 2015, refs. [3], [4], [8]).

reactions.name: Alkaline hydrolysis of arginine alpha-aminonitrile to arginine

conditions.other: simple alkaline hydrolysis in ammonia-rich pond or pool

reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis converts the alpha-aminonitrile into the alpha-amino acid arginine.
reactions.reasoning: The report explicitly states that simple alkaline hydrolysis of compound 46 would give arginine. This final transformation is mechanistically standard for Strecker-like aminonitrile to amino acid conversion, though the report also notes hydrolysis kinetics may be a bottleneck for accumulation (Patel et al., 2015; Pol et al., 2022).

reactions.name: Speculative hydrothermal reductive amination to glutamate

conditions.other: acidic to moderate pH reducing vent chemistry; included as metabolic-analog connector

reactions.mechanism: Fe-S-mediated reductive carbon fixation and amination furnish a C5 amino dicarboxylate analogue represented here as glutamate.
reactions.reasoning: This reaction is explicitly speculative. The supplied literature allows that glutamate may have been available in prebiotic ponds and that FeS/NiS surfaces can reductively aminate keto acids, but no direct prebiotic arginine route through glutamate has been demonstrated. Included only because Glavin et al. (2025) discuss glutamate as a conceivable feeder into Orn/Cit chemistry and Huber & Wachtershauser-type Fe-S amination supports general amino acid formation, not Arg specifically (Glavin et al., 2025; Huber & Wachtershauser, 1997 as summarized in ref. [9]).

reactions.name: Speculative transamination/decarboxylative conversion of glutamate to ornithine
reactions.mechanism: A metabolism-inspired sequence of transamination/reduction is used as an abstracted conversion from glutamate to ornithine.
reactions.reasoning: Explicitly speculative and included only because the literature mentions ornithine/citrulline as hypothesized intermediates by analogy to biology. No direct laboratory model for prebiotic Glu→Orn conversion was provided in the report (Glavin et al., 2025; Pol et al., 2022).

reactions.name: Surface generation of cyanate-like carbamoyl donor from cyanamide reservoir

conditions.other: UV-exposed shallow pool; included as carbamoyl-donor connector

reactions.mechanism: Cyanamide-rich nitrogen chemistry generates cyanate/isocyanate-like carbamoyl donors under surface photochemical conditions.
reactions.reasoning: This step is speculative but grounded in the report's statement that cyanate or cyanamide could act as carbamoyl/guanidinyl donors in hypothetical ornithine-to-citrulline chemistry. Added only to connect the literature-mentioned metabolism-analog branch (Glavin et al., 2025; Pol et al., 2022).

reactions.name: Speculative carbamoylation of ornithine to citrulline

conditions.other: ammonia-rich pond chemistry; undemonstrated prebiotic step

reactions.mechanism: Ornithine captures a carbamoyl equivalent to form citrulline.
reactions.reasoning: The report explicitly notes that prebiotic carbamoylation of ornithine using cyanate/cyanamide is a possible but unproven route. This reaction is therefore marked speculative and is not treated as evidence-equivalent to the Patel sequence (Glavin et al., 2025; Pol et al., 2022).

reactions.name: Speculative guanidinylation of citrulline to arginine

conditions.other: concentrated prebiotic pool; undemonstrated final guanidinium maturation

reactions.mechanism: A speculative ammonia/cyanamide-assisted guanidinylation completes conversion of citrulline-like precursor to arginine.
reactions.reasoning: This is explicitly speculative. The literature mentions that ornithine/citrulline could conceivably feed to arginine if prebiotic carbamoylation/guanidinylation existed, but no direct laboratory demonstration is known. Included only to represent the literature-mentioned open question branch while clearly distinguishing it from the Patel pathway (Glavin et al., 2025; Pol et al., 2022).

pathways.name: Canonical Patel cyanosulfidic route
pathways.description: Direct literature-supported surface synthesis from HCN, acetylene, NH3, H2S, and cyanamide through acrylonitrile, beta-aminopropionitrile, guanidinium hemiaminal, thiolactam, and compound 46 to arginine.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal sulfur-fed Patel route
pathways.description: Same Patel route but explicitly supplied by hydrothermal H2S exported to UV surface pools.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal ammonia-fed Patel route
pathways.description: Patel route with explicit hydrothermal provisioning of ammonia to surface nitrile chemistry.

pathways.name: Dual hydrothermal feed Patel route
pathways.description: Hydrothermal sulfur and ammonia jointly feed the surface cyanosulfidic sequence to arginine.

pathways.name: Formamide-derived cyanamide entry route
pathways.description: HCN is first routed through formamide to cyanamide before joining the Patel sequence at the guanidinium-installation stage.

pathways.name: Acrylonitrile-centered route with late cyanamide generation
pathways.description: Acrylonitrile and beta-aminopropionitrile are formed first, while cyanamide is generated in parallel from surface nitrogen chemistry before guanidinium capture.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-to-formamide-to-Patel hybrid
pathways.description: Hydrothermal ammonia and sulfur provision a surface network in which HCN is partially buffered through formamide/cyanamide before full Patel homologation.

pathways.name: Speculative glutamate-ornithine-citrulline route
pathways.description: A literature-acknowledged but undemonstrated metabolism-analog branch in which glutamate feeds ornithine and citrulline before arginine formation.

pathways.name: Hybrid Orn/Cit route supplied by Patel cyanamide chemistry
pathways.description: Speculative ornithine/citrulline path using cyanamide generated in the surface nitrogen network as the carbamoyl/guanidinyl donor.

pathways.name: Full connected network traversal to arginine
pathways.description: Longest integrated traversal showing hydrothermal feedstock generation, surface cyanamide reservoir formation, canonical Patel homologation, and final arginine release.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is the strongest pathway and aligns well with the literature-supported Patel et al. cyanosulfidic route. The sequence from acrylonitrile to beta-aminopropionitrile, cyanamide capture, sulfur-assisted photoreduction/homologation, and hydrolysis of the final aminonitrile to arginine is coherent and close to the only detailed prebiotic arginine proposal currently known. Confidence is reduced slightly because several late intermediates are summarized rather than chemically specified in full detail, and free arginine formation remains inferred by hydrolysis of compound 46 rather than demonstrated as a robust accumulation process in the broader environment.
suggestions: Explicitly specify UV wavelength, pH shifts, and Cu/Zn mediation for each Patel-sequence step
Clarify whether rxn_009 and rxn_010 represent single-step summaries of multi-operation reagent-pulse sequences
Document whether hydrolysis of compound 46 to arginine is experimentally shown or inferred under alkaline conditions

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The main arginine-forming branch remains strong, but the hydrothermal provisioning of H2S from CO2 and H2 is not chemically well supported as written. If rxn_002 is interpreted as environmental sourcing of sulfide from vent systems rather than net synthesis from listed reactants, the pathway becomes more defensible. The downstream Patel chemistry is good.
suggestions: Replace rxn_002 with a geochemically realistic sulfide source node or sulfur mineral dissolution process
Separate environmental transport/provisioning from true stoichiometric synthesis steps
Retain Patel late-stage sequence with more explicit step granularity

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway is acceptable only because the hydrothermal ammonia node is treated as feedstock provisioning. As an actual reaction, CO2 + H2 -> NH3 is not chemically valid without a nitrogen source. The surface sequence itself remains literature-aligned and is the reason this pathway is still near approval.
suggestions: Add N2, nitrite, or another plausible nitrogen source to ammonia-forming hydrothermal chemistry
Frame ammonia as a vent-associated available molecule rather than product of the stated stoichiometry
Preserve the downstream Patel route as the core target-forming branch

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Using both hydrothermal provisioning steps compounds the weakest part of the network. The central surface route is still strong and literature-consistent, but the entry assumptions are poorly specified chemically. The pathway is plausible as an environmental narrative, less so as a fully stoichiometric route.
suggestions: Revise hydrothermal reactions to realistic sulfur and nitrogen geochemistry
Distinguish source availability from actual synthesis chemistry
Keep the Patel branch as the validated core and reduce unsupported upstream transformations

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The alternative entry via HCN -> formamide -> cyanamide is weaker than the canonical Patel sequence because cyanamide production from formamide is not a standard literature-supported route in this context. Still, once cyanamide is available, the rest of the sequence is coherent and target-producing.
suggestions: Use a more established prebiotic cyanamide source or reservoir
Provide mineral and photochemical details supporting formamide conversion to cyanamide
Maintain this as an auxiliary feeder pathway rather than a primary route

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is a reasonable variant on the Patel route, but delayed cyanamide generation via formamide introduces the same weakness as P5. Acrylonitrile-centered progression is still chemically coherent and preserves the key literature-supported transformations.
suggestions: Provide a stronger cyanamide source
Clarify temporal sequencing to avoid incompatible one-pot assumptions
Annotate which operations are direct literature steps versus network-level summarizations

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This hybrid is overextended. It combines weak hydrothermal provisioning with a weak formamide-to-cyanamide feeder before entering the strong Patel route. As a connected network traversal it works, but as a chemically conservative synthesis pathway it is too speculative upstream.
suggestions: Simplify the route to avoid stacking speculative feeder steps
Anchor feedstock supply in known geochemical sources instead of unsupported stoichiometries
Use the canonical Patel route as the principal path

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This route is explicitly speculative and the chemistry is not literature-demonstrated. The glutamate-to-ornithine step is not a plausible single prebiotic conversion as stated, carbamoylation of ornithine via cyanate is hypothetical, and conversion of citrulline to arginine using ammonia/cyanamide is not established. It is acceptable as a hypothesis branch but not as an approved synthetic pathway.
suggestions: Keep this branch clearly labeled as metabolism-inspired speculation only
Do not treat it as evidence-equivalent to the Patel route
Require experimental support for ornithine carbamoylation and citrulline guanidinylation under prebiotic conditions

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Borrowing cyanamide chemistry from the Patel network does not rescue the speculative ornithine/citrulline branch. The core arginine-forming logic remains unsupported by direct experimental evidence, despite being connected to a stronger cyanamide reservoir.
suggestions: Demote this to a side hypothesis
Provide direct evidence for cyanamide-driven guanidinylation of citrulline or related intermediates
Avoid presenting this as a competitive alternative to P1

pathway_evaluations.feedback: As a whole-network traversal this is connected and narratively strong, showing hydrothermal-to-surface-to-biochemical flow. However, full traversal inherits all feeder weaknesses, especially unsupported hydrothermal provisioning and weak formamide-to-cyanamide conversion. Good as a network story, not the best chemically constrained pathway.
suggestions: Tighten feedstock geochemistry
Reduce dependence on unsupported feeder transformations
Treat canonical Patel steps as the validated backbone and other branches as optional context

evaluation.network_feedback: This is a strong network topologically and a reasonably good literature-grounded synthesis network overall, with the important caveat that only the Patel-style cyanosulfidic branch is genuinely well supported for arginine. The graph is connected, contains multiple hubs, and shows clear Hydrothermal -> Surface -> Biochemical progression. It also correctly reflects the literature conclusion that arginine is unusually difficult prebiotically and that the Patel route is the only detailed proposal. The major weaknesses are chemical overstatement in upstream hydrothermal provisioning reactions, inclusion of surface steps that do not map cleanly onto established cyanamide chemistry, and speculative metabolism-analog branches that are acknowledged but still weak. Another notable issue is compliance with the original generation requirements: the summarized network description does not specify mineral agents for every hydrothermal and surface reaction, and several hydrothermal stoichiometries are chemically invalid as written. Literature alignment is otherwise good because the network centers the Patel sequence and labels non-Patel routes speculative. Overall, the network is acceptable if judged as a synthesis network with one approved core pathway and several exploratory auxiliary branches.
